name: Informes 3r trimestre Camille Cappelle null.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                         Camille Cappelle
Teacher:                      Clarisse Buydens
Level:                        Emma 2024/2025
Absences:                     2
Frequency of use (audio):     Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation):   Very good
Comment:
Camille comes to class in a good mood and a smile on her
face. She participates enthusiastically in all the activities.
Camille truly loves story time. She likes reciting the story
with her classmates and her teacher. Camille also really
enjoys the "Numbers song", the "Can you please...?", the
"Happy face/sad face card game" and the "Where is Emma's
tattoo?" activities. Camille loves singing along to the songs
and she also shows a real interest in the worksheet activities.
Camille is always cooperative with her teacher and her
classmates.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process):   Good
Comment:
Camille's English knowledge is good. She knows a great
amount of vocabulary and structures. She can often use them
in sentences, sometimes requiring the teacher's help to do
so. This term, she specifically learnt the different actions and
the different rooms/places in which those actions take place
(e.g.: sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). She has
also reinforced her knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the
moods, the body parts and the food items.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):   Very good
Comment:
Camille is a joyful, attentive, friendly and bright student. She
treats her classmates and her teacher with respect and care.
She listens attentively in class and usually stays focused
during the lesson, seldom being distracted by her
classmates. On a few occasions, she jokingly kept the
material instead of giving it back to the teacher, but usually,
gives it back directly when asked. Moreover, she takes good
care of the material and always shares it with her
classmates.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:          7.20        Good. Camille can understand the question and answers either with a
                                      single word or with a structure which is only partially correct. The
decrease in her audio listening frequency might explain her good
result, instead of a very good one! The teacher still wants to
congratulate Camille on her good result! Keep up the good work!

---

Final comment

At the beginning of the school year, Camille was a very quiet and shy student. Thanks to her classmates’ friendliness
and her easy-going attitude, she came out of her shell and has felt comfortable in the class ever since. It has truly
been a joy to have Camille in class, thanks to her smiling and her good-humoured attitude. Furthermore, she has also
made progress in her English learning, developing both her memory and her comprehension skills.
It is recommended to listen to the audio tracks during the summer holidays, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary
and structures, learnt throughout the school year.

name: Informes 3r trimestre Luis Reves Raposo Very Good Emma.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                          Luis Reves Raposo
Teacher:                       Clarisse Buvdens
Level:                         Emma 2024/2025
Absences:                      0
Frequency of use (audio):      Pass

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation):   Very good
Comment:
Luis usually comes to class in a good and calm mood. He
usually participates in the activities with a certain
enthusiasm. Luis really likes story time and he takes an
active role in the storytelling, reciting the story with the
teacher. He also seemed to really enjoy the "Can you
please...?", the "Happy face/sad face card game", the "Where
can Emma...?" and the "Where can you...?" activities. Luis is
always cooperative with his classmates and his teacher.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process):   Very good
Comment:
Luis' English knowledge is very good. He knows a great
amount of vocabulary and structures. He can often use them
in sentences, sometimes requiring the teacher's help to do
so. This term, he specifically learnt the different actions and
the different rooms/places in which those actions take place
(e.g. sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). He has
also reinforced his knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the
moods, the body parts and the food items.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):   Very good
Comment:
Luis is a composed, gentle, friendly and attentive student. He
always treats his classmates and his teacher with respect
and care. He is seldom distracted by his classmates or
external elements. He usually stays focused and listens
attentively, following the teacher's instructions very well.
Luis, always treats the material with care, gives the material
back directly when asked, and shares it with his classmates.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:      8.40      Very good. Luis can understand the question and makes an effort to
use a complete structure in the answer, even if it is not always the
correct one.
Congratulations on your very good result, Luis! We're very proud of
you!

---

Final comment

Throughout the year, Luis has opened up and has seemed to really feel more comfortable with his classmates. It is a
real joy to have Luis in class, thanks to his diligent and mindful attitude, as well as his friendliness. Moreover, Luis
kept developing both his memory and comprehension skills. His oral test results have been in constant progress. Well
done, Luis! Furthermore, in order to keep in mind the vocabulary and structures learnt throughout the school year, it
is recommended to listen to the audio track during the summer holidays.

name: Informes 3r trimestre Rayan Duvivier  de Fortemps Acceptable Emma.pdf
content:
Kids&Us.

Objectives and content of the Emma course, 3rd term

---

Term report (3rd term)

Data

Name:                             Rayan Duvivier de Fortemps
Teacher:                          Clarisse Bydens
Level:                            Emma 2024/2025
Absences:                         0
Frequency of use (audio):         Insufficient

Participation/Motivation

Assessment (Participation / Motivation):   Very good
Comment:
Rayan usually comes to class in a cheerful and lively mood.
He participates in the activities with great enthusiasm. Rayan
does not seem overly interested in story time, usually losing
focus during that activity. However, he really enjoyed the
"Can you please...?", the "Happy face/sad face card game",
the "Where is Emma's tattoo?", the "Where can Emma...?"
and the "Where can you...?" activities. Rayan is usually quite
cooperative with his classmates and his teacher.

Learning process

Assessment (Learning process):             Satisfactory
Comment:
Rayan's English knowledge is satisfactory. He does know a
certain amount of vocabulary and structures. However, he
usually requires the teacher's help to repeat the full
structures and tell him how he must use words in sentences.
This term he specifically learnt the different actions and the
different rooms/places in which those actions take place
(e.g.: sleep in the bedroom, eat in the kitchen, etc.). He has
also reinforced his knowledge of the shapes, the colours, the
moods, the body parts and the food items.

Behaviour

Assessment (Behaviour):                 Good
Comment:
Rayan is a fun-loving, energetic and good-humoured student.
He seldom treats his classmates in an unfriendly manner,
generally treating them with respect and care. He also
generally treats his teacher in a respectful manner. Rayan
can still, at times, be distracted by external elements and his
classmates. However, he has learnt to stay more focused and
listen more attentively to the teacher, following the teacher's
instructions rather well. He tends to treat the material with
care and can give it back directly when asked, and usually
shares it with his classmates.

Marks

Oral Test Mark:                   6.20      Acceptable. Rayan can understand the question but, in most cases,
                                         still does not respond with a complete structure. The teacher had
                                         noticed a real improvement in Rayan's learning, when Rayan's audio
                                         listening frequency was more regular. He was able to understand
                                         what was expected of him during the activities and the meaning of the
                                         words used in the activities. Keep up the good work, Rayan!

---

Final comment

Throughout the school year and especially this term, Rayan’s behaviour has really improved. He still needs to move
around and explore his surroundings, but he does so in a calmer way. He has been getting better at listening to the
teacher and staying more focused. He has kept his grades up and with some work and a regular audio listening, his
English level can only increase! It is recommended to listen to the audio tracks during the summer holidays, in order
to keep in mind the vocabulary and structures learnt throughout the year.
